title: dalbergia retusa var. cuscatlanica
intro: Dalbergia retusa var. cuscatlanica, also known as the Cuscatlan rosewood, is a tree species native to Central America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Dalbergia retusa var. cuscatlanica include its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and wounds, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The bark is often used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
